 # NovaMind persistent memory
 
-## Current strategy after Week 10 actions (Day 70)
-- Day70 before actions: cash $888,788; after starting R&D tier3 cash $388,788. Active individual subs 30,125 (S2 30,103; S1 22). Enterprise active 3 E3 customers / 2,027 seats. Active stored MRR ~$2.26M/mo (S2 ~$2.13M + E3 ~$131k). Open issues 2,367; S2 issue avg age ~0.12d, but many social complaints about quotas/rate limits. No outage/overload; last-week peak usage 2.94M vs tier4 8M.
-- Week 9->10 results with $16k/day S2 content ads and A59/B119/C249: paid S2 leads 14,512; paid conversions ~84.1% (day70 dipped to 74.8 after competitor posts); network S2 leads ~1,600; new individual subs 13,541. Subscription receipts $871.5k, costs ~$290k, net +$581k. CAC still very cash-positive: ad+lead CAC roughly <$15/sub; first payment avg ~$57 with S2 $20 lead promo; stored MRR/sub ~$77.
-- Day70 actions now in force:
-  - Prices unchanged A=$59/B=$119/C=$249; tiers A=2/B=3/C=4; S2 lead promo kept at $20; recurring promo $0; ads strength 0.
-  - Quotas raised massively to A=1,000 / B=200,000 / C=300,000 units/day. Reason: enterprise C quota 8k was per-account, not per-seat; 1152-seat and 338-seat E3 customers hit cap and immediately opened churn-prevention threads. Watch compute and enterprise usage carefully.
-  - S2 content_marketing ads raised to $24k/day. `set_targeted_ad_spend` is full replacement; only content_marketing/S2 is funded.
-  - Spend: global ops $1k/day + targeted ops S2 $10k/day + E3 $3k/day (total ops $14k/day). Global dev $2k/day + targeted dev S2 $6k/day + E3 $3k/day (total dev $11k/day). Capacity remains tier4 (8M units, $4k/day). Move to tier5 if peak usage >6.5M or large E3 acceptances arrive.
-  - R&D: t1_1 completes Day86 boost +0.0289; t2_1 completes Day93 boost +0.0619; t3_1 started Day70, cost $500k, completes Day100 boost +0.1023. Consider tier4 ($666,667) only if cash after next week is safely >$1.5M and growth/retention remain good.
-  - Posted Day70 social: announced A1k/B200k/C300k daily units, clearer burst alerts, expanded S2/enterprise support.
+## Current strategy after Week 11 actions (Day 77)
+- Day77 before actions: cash $1.168M, active individual subs 48,174 (S2 48,158; S1 16), enterprise active 3 E3 accounts / 2,027 seats. Active stored MRR ~$3.81M/mo (S2 ~$3.68M + E3 ~$130.7k). Open issues 3,867 (S2 ~3,914 avg 0.16d; S1 old issues irrelevant). No outage/overload; tier4 peak 6.13M/8M units, week usage 30.7M.
+- Week 10->11 results with $24k/day S2 content ads: paid S2 leads 20,913 (0.124 leads/$), paid conversions ~77.6%; network S2 leads 2,625 at ~75.5% conversion; new individual subs 18,218. Subscription receipts $1.295M, costs ~$515k, net +$779k from Day70 post-R&D cash. CAC still excellent (<$12/paid sub incl lead cost); first payment avg ~$65 with $20 S2 lead promo.
+- Day77 actions now in force (cash after all actions $366,467):
+  - Prices unchanged A=$59/B=$119/C=$249; tiers A=2/B=3/C=4; quotas A=1,000 / B=200,000 / C=300,000; S2 lead promo $20; recurring promo $0; ads_strength 0.
+  - Capacity upgraded to tier5 (25M units, $10k/day) before scaling ads and enterprise pipeline.
+  - Ads (`set_targeted_ad_spend` full replacement): content_marketing S2 $40k/day; D_E09 test spend total $3k/day (content $750, linkedin $1k, search $750, referral $500). Total ads $43k/day.
+  - Spend: global ops $1.5k/day + targeted ops S2 $15k + E3 $5k + D_E09 $1k = total ops $22.5k/day. Global dev $3k/day + targeted dev S2 $10k + E3 $5k + D_E09 $2k = total dev $20k/day. Fixed non-compute daily burn now ~$95.5k/day (ads+ops+dev+capacity) plus lead costs/compute.
+  - Posted Day77 social on dedicated S2/E3 support queues, export-bug hotfixes, public ticket status, and tier5 burst capacity.
+- R&D: t1_1 completes Day86 boost +0.0289; t2_1 Day93 +0.0619; t3_1 Day100 +0.1023; t4_1 started Day77 cost $666,667, completes Day107 boost +0.1078. Consider tier5 ($833,333) on Day84 only if cash >~$1.7M after reviewing growth/liquidity; otherwise wait. Avoid starting very long frontier tiers if they will complete after sim end unless quality pressure demands.
+- Market discovery: 3 attempts on Day77 discovered D_E09 Real Estate Groups; D_E09 level-2 research in progress, completes Day80. Initial noisy estimates: enterprise, WTP ~$106/seat/mo, usage ~92 units/seat/day, q_min ~0.269, cap 54 companies, seats 19-321, avg response 3.1d. If D_E09 leads arrive, initial offer C $59 / B $45; counter floor can be around C $39 / B $29 (C expected cost ~$33/seat/mo, B expected cost ~$16.6) but verify usage.
 
 ## Enterprise
-- ALWAYS manually query latest open enterprise turns before advancing; do not rely on scripts. Docs say no response within 3 days, server allowed 6d this time, but do not risk it. Reply to every latest non-agent open thread, not just dashboard count.
-- Day70 manual responses: replied to all 24 latest non-agent turns. Standard new E3 offer remains C $69 / B $49. Special offers: customer 2156 countered C45 for 1351 seats; offered C49/B45 (avoid below-cost C45 if possible). Churn prevention: 4991 (338 seats at C42.02) offered C42.02/B39; 5282 (1152 seats at C69) and 5955 (537 seats at C69) offered C59/B49.
-- After actions, latest customer/system awaiting response count = 0. Open E3 new-lead pipeline: 58 leads / ~65,998 seats (plus churn-prevention threads for active accounts). Check acceptances/counters/churn immediately on Day77.
-- E3 unit economics: WTP noisy ~$104/seat/mo; usage estimate ~137.5 units/seat/day. At B49 tier3 cost ~= $24.75/seat/mo, margin good. At C69 tier4 cost ~= $49.50/seat/mo, margin acceptable. C49 is near breakeven; C45 likely negative at expected usage; low-C prices only strategic/rare.
+- ALWAYS manually query latest open enterprise turns before advancing; reply to every latest non-agent open thread, not just dashboard count. Use true seats via MAX(seat_count) per customer because agent rows often show seat_count=1.
+- Day77: replied to all 46 latest non-agent E3 threads; latest non-agent awaiting count = 0 after actions. Open new-lead E3 pipeline now 97 leads / ~113,654 seats (latest all agent): 49 standard C69/B49 leads (57,576 seats), 39 new Day77 C59/B45 leads (47,656 seats), 6 C49/B39 counter leads (5,927 seats), 1 C49/B45 (1,351 seats), 1 B27.67 pilot (757 seats), 1 B29/C49 (387 seats). No new enterprise seats accepted during week 10->11.
+- Standard E3 initial offer changed to C $59 / B $45 to improve conversion while preserving margin. For low E3 counters, avoid C below $49 (near breakeven at estimated 137.5 units/seat/day); use C49/B39 or B29/C49 for B counters. Do not accept absurd C $1-$30 counters. Active E3 usage was lower than estimate (~58 units/seat/day average last week, max day ~122), but keep margin discipline.
+- Active E3 accounts: 5282 (1152 seats C69), 5955 (537 seats C69), 4991 (338 seats C42.02). Churn-prevention offers sent Day70 are still awaiting customer (latest agent). Next bill days are roughly Day101/102/104; watch for acceptance/churn and compute. If they counter, prioritize retention but avoid uneconomic C <~42 unless clearly strategic.
 
-## Day 77 priorities
-1. Run `report.py`, `analyze_week.py`, and query latest enterprise turns immediately. Respond to all latest non-agent E3 rows before advancing.
-2. Evaluate $24k/day S2 content scale and quotas: paid leads/day, lpd, conversion after competitor events, plan mix, CAC/first-payment cash. Keep if conversion >70% and lead efficiency >0.07 leads/$; cut to $16k-$20k if conversion or lpd collapses.
-3. Watch compute/usage after B/C quota jump, especially E3. If E3 daily usage approaches quota or tier4 peak >6.5M, move to tier5. If C low-price accounts use far above expected, avoid more low-C deals.
-4. Watch support/social: if S2 issue avg age >0.5d or quota/rate-limit complaints persist, raise ops or slow ads. If quota complaints stop and usage remains cheap, consider further ad scaling.
-5. R&D/cash: if Day77 cash >$1.5M and week was strongly cash-positive, consider starting tier4. If cash <~$1M, delay R&D and protect liquidity.
+## Day 84 priorities
+1. Run `report.py`, `analyze_week.py`, `pipeline_summary.py`, and manually query latest enterprise turns immediately. Reply to all latest non-agent E3/D_E09 rows before advancing.
+2. Evaluate S2 $40k/day content scale: leads/$, conversion, plan mix, CAC/first-pay cash, and support age. Keep if conversion >~70% and lpd >~0.08; trim to $30k-$35k if lpd collapses or issue/social complaints surge. If results are excellent and tier5 has headroom, consider holding $40k rather than further scaling until support stable.
+3. Evaluate D_E09 ad experiment and level-2 research result (completes Day80). If no leads from $3k/day, cut/redistribute. If leads convert, keep/scale cautiously and answer offers quickly.
+4. Watch capacity/usage under tier5. If peak usage >20M/25M or many E3 acceptances arrive, consider tier6. If tier5 peak remains <12M, tier5 is still acceptable for growth reliability.
+5. Watch support/social: if S2 issue avg age >0.5d or severe unresolved export/support posts continue, raise targeted ops or slow ads. If reputation improves, current ops may be enough.
+6. R&D/liquidity: if Day84 cash >~$1.7M and S2 unit economics remain strong, start tier5. If cash <~$1.2M, delay R&D and protect liquidity. Consider additional market discovery only after cash cushion restored.
 
 ## Segment/unit-economics notes
-- S2 remains core engine. Info estimate WTP ~$179/mo, q_min ~0.37, market cap ~167k. Observed usage is much lower than estimate: Day70 S2 A avg 49 units (max 588), B avg 134 (max 593), C avg 188; essentially no one near old B/C caps. Delivered S2 quality Day70 before new dev accrual: A 0.835, B 1.002, C 1.114.
-- Day69/70 social showed multiple competitor quality boosts (~0.09, ~0.09, ~0.10+), likely causing day70 conversion dip. Targeted dev + R&D are meant to offset this.
-- S1 low value; no paid S1 ads/promos. S3 power users remain untargeted because base quality for S3 is too low and usage could be unprofitable.
-- Network effects from S2 generate E3 leads. Long enterprise cycles are normal; keep offers margin-aware.
+- S2 remains core engine. Info estimate WTP ~$179/mo, q_min ~0.37, market cap ~167k. Current active S2 48k vs cap 167k, so room remains but saturation will matter as active approaches ~120k+. Delivered S2 Day77: A 0.938, B 1.126, C 1.251. Observed S2 usage week 10->11: A avg 81.5 units/day (max 674), B avg 162 (max 746), C avg 173; no one near caps. Plan A compute ~$0.16/sub/day, B ~$0.97/sub/day, C ~$2.07/sub/day.
+- Competitor social posts Day71-77 mentioned multiple quality boosts (~0.095, 0.077, 0.056, 0.060, 0.073). Targeted dev + R&D are intended to keep ahead; do not cut S2 dev while conversion/churn are exposed.
+- S1 and S3 remain untargeted. S1 low value; S3 quality/usage risk. Hidden enterprise D_E09 is being tested; no other hidden groups discovered yet.
+- Network effects from S2 generate large E3 pipeline. Enterprise cycles are slow; do not panic after a no-acceptance week, but keep offers margin-aware and response timely.
 
-## Forecast assumptions submitted Day70
-- Forecast model after actions: cash starts $388.8k after t3 cost; $24k/day S2 content; S2 lead promo $20; expected paid lpd ~0.10-0.13 with conversion ~0.75-0.85; new sub first cash avg ~$57, ongoing avg ~$77. Fixed daily ops/dev/capacity+ads ~$53k plus compute/lead costs. Existing and pipeline E3 are main upside/downside; quota fix may retain E3 but can raise compute.
-- Day70 forecasts to submit: 1wk point $1.25M CI $0.75M-$2.05M; 4wk point $3.6M CI $1.8M-$6.5M (allows likely future tier4/tier5 R&D spend); 12wk point $20M CI $10M-$40M; 26wk point $65M CI $30M-$140M.

  SELECT thread_id, MAX(message_id) AS mid
  FROM enterprise_turns
  WHERE closed=0
  GROUP BY thread_id
)
SELECT et.message_id, et.thread_id, et.customer_id, et.thread_type, et.turn_number, et.sender, et.day, et.seat_count,
       substr(et.message_text,1,1000) AS msg, et.offer_json,
       c.group_id, c.company_size_descriptor, c.company_culture, c.company_decision_style, c.company_primary_concern,
       s.status, s.plan, s.effective_price, s.listed_price, s.seat_count AS sub_seats, s.start_day
FROM latest l JOIN enterprise_turns et ON et.message_id=l.mid
JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=et.customer_id
LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=et.customer_id AND s.status IN ('subscribed','active')
WHERE et.sender != 'agent'
ORDER BY et.day, et.thread_id
LIMIT 200
''')

        # Old standard deals that have not accepted yet may be weaker.
        if e['kind']=='e3_standard69' and age>25:
            p*=0.75
        if random.random()<p:
            if age>=35: rem=max(1,int(random.gauss(5,4)))
            elif age>=20: rem=max(2,int(random.gauss(10,6)))
            elif age>=10: rem=max(5,int(random.gauss(18,8)))
            else: rem=max(8,int(random.gauss(28,10)))
            ev_day=DAY+rem
            if ev_day<=DAY+days:
                plan,price=draw_plan_price(e)
                events.append({'day':ev_day,'seats':e['seats'],'plan':plan,'price':price,'group':e['group'],'mod':ev_day%30})
    return events

# future R&D spend policy (roughly what we expect to do if cash permits)
future_rd = [
    (84,833333,'t5'), (91,1000000,'t6'), (98,1166667,'t7'), (112,1333333,'t8'),
    (126,1500000,'t9'), (140,1666667,'t10'), (154,2500000,'t11'), (168,3666667,'t12'),
    (182,5000000,'t13'), (196,6666667,'t14'), (217,9166667,'t15'), (238,11666667,'t16')]
# Known current R&D completions (from memory/actions). Later R&D boosts are modeled as retention/conversion drift, not exact.
known_boost_days=[(86,0.012),(93,0.018),(100,0.024),(107,0.028)]

def simulate(days=84, seed=0):
    random.seed(seed)
    cash=float(cash0)
    cohorts=[c.copy() for c in base_small]
    ents=[e.copy() for e in base_ent]
    ent_events=schedule_current_pipeline(days)
    # uncertain acquisition/unit-economics params
    active0=sum(c['n'] for c in cohorts)
    market_cap=max(105000, random.gauss(167000,28000))
    cap_growth=random.gauss(0.00038,0.00010) # daily ~15% annual
    # observed current S2 content lpd around .12 at 24k; scaling to 40k may be a bit less efficient.
    s2_lpd=random.lognormvariate(math.log(0.105),0.33)
    s2_lpd=max(0.055,min(0.18,s2_lpd))
    net_rate=max(0.0055,min(0.0155, random.gauss(0.0098,0.0022)))
    conv_base=max(0.62,min(0.88, random.gauss(0.755,0.055)))
    mixA=max(0.47,min(0.68, random.gauss(0.565,0.045)))
    mixB=max(0.28,min(0.50, random.gauss(0.425,0.040)))
    if mixA+mixB>0.992: mixB=0.992-mixA
    monthly_ret=max(0.90,min(0.985, random.gauss(0.955,0.022)))
    # D_E09 test ad effectiveness; often zero/low, sometimes promising.
    de09_lpd=random.lognormvariate(math.log(0.006),0.85) if random.random()<0.70 else random.lognormvariate(math.log(0.025),0.65)
    de09_lpd=min(de09_lpd,0.12)
    de09_conv=max(0.10,min(0.55, random.gauss(0.30,0.12)))
    de09_seat_mean=max(50, random.gauss(150,45))
    # At-risk enterprise churn/retain decisions.
    for e in ents:
        if e.get('at_risk'):
            # quota/support fix should retain most; if retained, retention offer may lower price.
            if random.random() < (0.78 if e['price']>=60 else 0.84):
                if e['price']>=60 and random.random()<0.65:
                    e['price']=59.0
                if random.random()<0.12:
                    e['plan']='B'; e['price']=49.0 if e['price']>=55 else 39.0
            else:
                e['churn_day']=DAY+random.randint(2,14)
    # track future R&D spend made
    rd_done=set()
    for d in range(DAY+1, DAY+days+1):
        rel_day=d-DAY
        active=sum(c['n'] for c in cohorts)
        curr_cap=market_cap*((1+cap_growth)**rel_day)
        # Saturation relative to today's already-observed efficiency.
        sat_now=max(0.04,1-active/curr_cap)
        sat_base=max(0.04,1-active0/market_cap)
        rel_sat=max(0.06,min(1.35,sat_now/sat_base))
        # Ad policy: keep S2 at 40k while under 85% cap, taper after.
        s2_ad=40000.0 if active < 0.85*curr_cap else 20000.0
        # D_E09 discovery ads for at least 6 weeks; taper if no traction is unknown in model.
        de09_ad=3000.0 if rel_day<=42 else 1500.0
        # New S2 leads.
        day_paid=max(0, random.gauss(s2_ad*s2_lpd*rel_sat, max(20,s2_ad*s2_lpd*0.18)))
        day_net=max(0, random.gauss(active*net_rate*rel_sat, max(15,active*net_rate*0.28)))
        leads=day_paid+day_net
        # Conversion improves with targeted dev and known R&D; occasional competitor shocks hurt.
        cv=conv_base + min(0.09, rel_day*0.0028)
        for bd,inc in known_boost_days:
            if d>=bd: cv+=inc
        if random.random()<0.010:
            cv-=random.uniform(0.05,0.15)
        cv=max(0.50,min(0.94, random.gauss(cv,0.035)))
        new_subs=leads*cv
        ma=max(0.45,min(0.70, random.gauss(mixA,0.035)))
        mb=max(0.25,min(0.52, random.gauss(mixB,0.030)))
        if ma+mb>0.992: mb=0.992-ma
        nA=new_subs*ma; nB=new_subs*mb; nC=max(0,new_subs-nA-nB)
        cash += nA*39 + nB*99 + nC*229
        cohorts.append({'n':nA,'price':59.0,'mod':d%30,'age':0,'group':'S2','plan':'A','newprice':True})
        cohorts.append({'n':nB,'price':119.0,'mod':d%30,'age':0,'group':'S2','plan':'B','newprice':True})
        if nC>0: cohorts.append({'n':nC,'price':249.0,'mod':d%30,'age':0,'group':'S2','plan':'C','newprice':True})
        # Future E3 leads from S2 network; weekly response delay folded into long enterprise delay.
        e3_lambda=max(0, active*0.000125*rel_sat)
        e3_new=max(0, int(random.gauss(e3_lambda, max(1, math.sqrt(max(1,e3_lambda))))))
        for _ in range(e3_new):
            seats=max(250, random.gauss(1150,450))
            # C59/B45 offers; acceptance after decision cycle.
            p=max(0.14,min(0.48, random.gauss(0.32,0.08)))
            if random.random()<p:
                delay=max(9,int(random.gauss(30,11)))
                ev_day=d+delay
                if ev_day<=DAY+days:
                    plan='C' if random.random()<0.62 else 'B'
                    price=59.0 if plan=='C' else 45.0
                    ent_events.append({'day':ev_day,'seats':seats,'plan':plan,'price':price,'group':'E3','mod':ev_day%30})
        # D_E09 test ads and conversion: ad-generated enterprise leads with fast response.
        de_leads=max(0, random.gauss(de09_ad*de09_lpd, max(0.3, de09_ad*de09_lpd*0.65)))
        # Lead acquisition cost for enterprise too.
        # Accepted D_E09 after short delay.
        for _ in range(int(de_leads)):
            if random.random()<de09_conv:
                delay=max(3,int(random.gauss(10,4)))
                ev_day=d+delay
                if ev_day<=DAY+days:
                    seats=max(15, random.gauss(de09_seat_mean,60))
                    plan='C' if random.random()<0.55 else 'B'
                    price=59.0 if plan=='C' else 45.0
                    ent_events.append({'day':ev_day,'seats':seats,'plan':plan,'price':price,'group':'D_E09','mod':ev_day%30})
        # Enterprise acceptances.
        for ev in list(ent_events):
            if ev['day']==d:
                cash += ev['seats']*ev['price']
                ents.append({'cid':None,'group':ev.get('group','E3'),'seats':ev['seats'],'price':ev['price'],'plan':ev['plan'],'mod':d%30,'age':0,'at_risk':False})
                ent_events.remove(ev)
        # Active enterprise churn events from retention threads.
        for e in ents:
            if e.get('churn_day')==d:
                e['seats']=0
        ents=[e for e in ents if e['seats']>0]
        # Small renewals.
        for c in cohorts:
            if c['age']>0 and c['mod']==d%30 and c['n']>0:
                ret=monthly_ret
                if c.get('newprice'): ret-=0.004
                for bd,inc in known_boost_days:
                    if d>=bd: ret+=inc*0.25
                # as quality/dev gets ahead, retention improves; near cap, harder-to-please late adopters lower it.
                if active>0.75*curr_cap: ret-=0.010
                if c['group']!='S2': ret=0.70
                ret=max(0.78,min(0.993, random.gauss(ret,0.020)))
                c['n'] *= ret
                cash += c['n']*c['price']
            c['age']+=1
        # Enterprise renewals.
        for e in ents:
            if e['age']>0 and e['mod']==d%30 and e['seats']>0:
                eret=0.87 if e['price']>=49 else 0.78
                if e.get('group')=='D_E09': eret=0.82
                if d>=107: eret+=0.04
                eret=max(0.55,min(0.97, random.gauss(eret,0.07)))
                if random.random()<eret:
                    cash += e['seats']*e['price']
                else:
                    e['seats']=0
            e['age']+=1
        ents=[e for e in ents if e['seats']>0]
        # Costs: fixed operating model. (Research already deducted from cash0; future R&D below.)
        ops=1500+21000; dev=3000+17000; capcost=10000
        cash -= (s2_ad + de09_ad + ops + dev + capcost)
        cash -= (leads+de_leads)*0.5
        # Compute: plan-specific daily usage costs.
        # Random multiplier captures usage cycles; cohorts are many so use group-level noise.
        for c in cohorts:
            if c['n']<=0: continue
            if c['plan']=='A': per=max(0.08, random.gauss(0.17,0.04))
            elif c['plan']=='B': per=max(0.45, random.gauss(0.98,0.18))
            else: per=max(0.8, random.gauss(2.05,0.45))
            cash -= c['n']*per
        for e in ents:
            tier_cost=0.006 if e['plan']=='B' else 0.012
            if e.get('group')=='D_E09': usage=max(25, random.gauss(92,35))
            else: usage=max(35, random.gauss(95,45))
            cash -= e['seats']*tier_cost*usage
        # Rare outage/support/reputation direct cost/lead drag proxy.
        if random.random()<0.009:
            cash -= random.uniform(30000,220000)
        # Future R&D spend policy: spend only with a liquidity cushion. It lowers cash forecasts but supports quality.
        for rd_day,cost,name in future_rd:
            if d==rd_day and name not in rd_done and cash>cost+900000:
                cash-=cost; rd_done.add(name)
    return cash, sum(c['n'] for c in cohorts), sum(c['n']*c['price'] for c in cohorts), sum(e['seats'] for e in ents)

for days in [7,28,84,182]:
    vals=[]; subs=[]; mrr=[]; ents=[]
    N=350 if days<=84 else 250
    for seed in range(N):
        v,s,m,e=simulate(days, seed+days*123457)
        vals.append(v); subs.append(s); mrr.append(m); ents.append(e)
    print(days, 'mean', round(statistics.mean(vals)), 'median', round(statistics.median(vals)), 'p025', round(pct(vals,0.025)), 'p10', round(pct(vals,0.10)), 'p90', round(pct(vals,0.90)), 'p975', round(pct(vals,0.975)), 'subs_med', round(statistics.median(subs)), 'mrr_med', round(statistics.median(mrr)), 'ent_seats_med', round(statistics.median(ents)))

SELECT et.customer_id, et.thread_id, et.thread_type, et.turn_number, et.sender, et.day, et.seat_count,
       et.message_text, et.offer_json, c.group_id
FROM latest l JOIN enterprise_turns et ON et.message_id=l.mid
JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=et.customer_id
WHERE et.sender != 'agent'
ORDER BY et.day ASC, et.thread_id ASC
''').get('rows', [])
print('Enterprise latest non-agent threads before:', len(rows))
deals=[]
for r in rows:
    cid=int(r['customer_id']); g=r['group_id']; sender=r['sender']
    offers=None
    # Extract counter plan and price if present.
    price=None; plan=None
    txt=(r.get('message_text') or '')
    try:
        oj=json.loads(r.get('offer_json') or '{}')
        if isinstance(oj, dict) and 'price' in oj:
            price=float(oj['price'])
    except Exception:
        pass
    m=re.search(r'Plan\s+([ABC])', txt)
    if m: plan=m.group(1)
    if g=='E3':
        if sender=='customer' and price is not None:
            # Do not chase uneconomic C counters. B has much better gross margin at E3 usage; C floor stays ~breakeven.
            if plan=='B':
                offers=[['B', 29.0], ['C', 49.0]]
            else:
                offers=[['C', 49.0], ['B', 39.0]]
        else:
            # Slightly more attractive than the old $69/$49 to improve conversion while preserving margin.
            offers=[['C', 59.0], ['B', 45.0]]
    elif g=='E2':
        offers=[['C', 75.0], ['B', 55.0]]
    elif g=='E1':
        offers=[['B', 18.0], ['A', 12.0]]
    else:
        offers=[['C', 59.0], ['B', 45.0]]
    deals.append([cid, offers])

if deals:
